evidence_quotes.quote: Video games, in the current times, occupy a large margin in total annual sales of such products.
no company should have the right to remove the goods we bought

The responsibility for retaining the legal copy of a video game acquired by a private individual lies with that person: this has always been in physical format and should be the same as the digital one.
there is no remote possibility that many video games can now disappear, literally being destroyed by their publishers, without any compensation being paid to the consumer
video games are art, no more or less, and art deserves to be preserved

paragraphs.text: To the members of this Committee: Video games, in the current times, occupy a large margin in total annual sales of such products. It is not unreasonable to expect a certain level of consumer protection when we buy digital video games: no company should have the right to remove the goods we bought and, not because they are digital goods, it is logical to think the opposite. The responsibility for retaining the legal copy of a video game acquired by a private individual lies with that person: this has always been in physical format and should be the same as the digital one. In addition, there is no remote possibility that many video games can now disappear, literally being destroyed by their publishers, without any compensation being paid to the consumer, or by no means of continuing to use that video game for their personal use, is absolutely tragic and extremely unjust. Please consider including digital video games as something worth preserving; video games are art, no more or less, and art deserves to be preserved, both by pride and satisfaction of its creators, and by basic rights of those who consume these products. Thank you in advance for the opportunity offered to give my most sincere opinion.
